question_text: Consider the reaction conditions and spectroscopic data and predict the structures of compounds A and B. Reagents: Starting compound (tetrahydro-2H-pyran-2-yl)oxy)propyl ketone reacted with Ph3P-CH3(+) Br(-) and NaH (1 equiv) to form Compound A and Compound B. IR, MS, and 13C NMR data are provided for compounds A and B. 1H NMR spectra show specific multiplets, doublets, singlets with coupling constants and D2O exchange information. Identify the structures of compounds A and B (two chemical structure diagrams given: Compound A is HOCH2CH2CH=CH2, Compound B is CH2=CHCOMe).
canonical_answer: Compound A = HOCCCCC=CH2 (HOCH2CH2CH=CH2); Compound B = CH2=CHCOMe (allyl methyl ketone)

error_comment: Task requires drawing and is not text-oriented

question_text:
Predict the principal products expected (if any) for the following reaction sequences. Provide accurate depictions of stereochemical outcomes. For each, draw the structure you want to be evaluated in the box provided.

A) Reaction of an aldehyde with Me group and an enone (with methyl and alkene groups) with pyrrolidine. Hint: A ring is formed in this reaction.

B) Reaction of a bicyclic ketone with Ph3P=CHOCH3 followed by ethylene glycol and catalytic acid.

C) Reaction of a methoxy-substituted sugar alcohol with catalytic acid and CH3OH.

D) Reaction of a polyhydroxycyclohexanone with NaIO4, H2O, MeOH followed by MeOH with catalytic acid under reflux.

E) Reaction of a Boc-protected amine with two alkenes with (PCy3)2Cl2Ru=CHPh catalyst in CH2Cl2, then mCPBA oxidation in CH2Cl2, followed by reaction with Ph2Cu(CN)Li2 in THF at -20 °C, and finally TFA in CH2Cl2.
canonical_answer: A) racemic C13H20O structure shown; B) bicyclic ether with ethylene glycol acetal; C) methoxy acetal of sugar C8H16O3 structure shown; D) methylated product with ester C11H20O4 structure shown; E) racemic hydroxylated pyrrolidine with Ph substituent

question_text: Compound C has the molecular formula C8H17N. Treatment of C with excess methyl iodide, followed by silver oxide and heating, gives the pure (S)-enantiomer of N,N-dimethyloct-7-en-3-amine. Propose a stereochemically accurate structure for compound C and describe the curved arrow mechanism to show how this reaction gives the observed product. Both diastereomers of compound C are capable of undergoing Hofmann Elimination, but the diastereomer shown on the left is more stable. The more important stereocenter is the carbon bearing the ethyl group since that must correspond to forming the (S)-enantiomer of the product as detailed in the prompt. Also, since the rate of Hofmann elimination is CH3 > CH2 > CH at positions β to NR4+, the terminal olefin is formed selectively.
canonical_answer: Stereochemically accurate structure of compound C as shown in boxed structure with correct stereochemistry and curved arrow mechanism as shown in detailed reaction scheme

question_text: Redraw each of the following sugars in open-chain form as a Fischer projection, using the standard convention for depicting carbohydrates. Choose the letters associated with D sugars. Sugars shown with the letters A, B, and C with their cyclic structures are provided, and the corresponding Fischer projections are labeled A, B, and C.

error_comment: Task requires prediction of chemical structures or missing reagents from complex multi-step synthesis involving reaction schemes and structures, which is a drawing-based task.

question_text: Crellinose is a reducing disaccharide which undergoes mutarotation. Hydrolysis by either aqueous acid or alpha-arabinosidases give L-arabinose and D-glucose. Oxidation of crellinose with bromine water followed by acid hydrolysis gives L-arabinose and D-gluconic acid. Methylation of the bromine-water oxidation product, followed by acid hydrolysis gives 2,3,4-tri-O-methyl-L-arabinose and 2,3,4,5-tetra-O-methyl-D-gluconic acid. Methylation and acid hydrolysis of crellinose gives 2,3,4-tri-O-methyl-L-arabinose and 2,3,4-tri-O-methyl-D-glucose. What is the structure of crellinose? Draw your representation using appropriate Haworth structures. Show your logic and explain the experimental data.
canonical_answer: The structure of crellinose consists of a pyranose L-arabinose linked by alpha-1,6-linkage to a pyranose D-glucose.

question_text: The complete structure of JZ, a polypeptide with antibiotic properties, has been worked out as follows: I) Analysis of the hydrolysis products gave equimolar amounts of Leu, Orn, Phe, Pro, Val. (Ornithine, Orn, is a rare amino acid whose formula is shown: H2N-(CH2)3-CH(NH2)-CO2H.) II) Measurement of the molecular weight gave an approximate value of 1250. III) Sanger N-terminal residue analysis with 1-fluoro-2,4-dinitrobenzene gave only DNB-NHCH2CH2CH2CH(NH3+)CO2-. IV) Partial hydrolysis of JZ gave the following di- and tri-peptides: Leu-Phe, Orn-Leu, Phe-Pro, Val-Orn, Phe-Pro-Val, Pro-Val-Orn, Val-Orn-Leu. Explain the results of the Sanger experiment (statement III) and determine the structure of JZ. Write the structure using three-letter amino acid representations, and draw a box around it to indicate your proposed structure for evaluation.
canonical_answer: The results of the Sanger experiment indicate that no N terminus is present in the polypeptide since the FDNB did not react with any free alpha-amino groups other than the one on the Orn side chain. The proposed structure in three-letter code is: Orn-Leu-Phe-Pro-Val-Orn-Leu-Phe-Pro-Val-Orn forming a cyclic sequence as shown.

question_text:
Outline a synthesis of the following compound starting from benzene, toluene, or aniline. You have at your disposal any reagents encountered in Ch 41a, b, or c, and may use any organic molecules containing four carbons or fewer. The target compound is a nitro-substituted chalcone derivative with the nitro group para to the alpha,beta-unsaturated ketone. The synthesis scheme includes the following steps:
- Starting from toluene, nitration with HNO3 to give para-nitrotoluene,
- Oxidation with KMnO4 to p-nitrobenzoic acid,
- Reaction 1) SOCl2, 2) LiAlH(OtBu)3 to p-nitrobenzaldehyde,
- Starting from benzene, reaction with acetyl chloride to acetophenone,
- Reaction with LDA and p-nitrobenzaldehyde followed by heating to give the target product.
canonical_answer: Outline includes nitration of toluene with HNO3; oxidation with KMnO4 to p-nitrobenzoic acid; conversion to p-nitrobenzaldehyde via SOCl2 and LiAlH(OtBu)3; Friedel-Crafts acetylation of benzene with acetyl chloride to acetophenone; forming the alpha,beta-unsaturated ketone via LDA and condensation with p-nitrobenzaldehyde followed by heating.

question_text:
Propose a synthesis of the following molecule from succinic acid. You have at your disposal any reagents encountered in Ch 41a, b, or c, and may use any organic molecules containing four carbons or fewer. (Hint: A good synthetic route takes advantage of the symmetry present in the target molecule.) The synthesis scheme includes:
- Starting from succinic acid,
- Reaction with 2 equivalents of 2-hydroxyethyl (vinyl alcohol) under acidic conditions (TsOH · H2O or any other acid) to give a diester intermediate,
- Treatment with 2 equivalents NaH (or any base) followed by aqueous acidic work-up,
- Methylation with 2 equivalents of MeI and 2 equivalents of K2CO3 (or any other base) to give the target molecule.
It is noted that unmethylated diketone is also acceptable as a product.
canonical_answer: Esterification of succinic acid with 2 equivalents 2-hydroxyethyl vinyl ether using acid catalyst (TsOH · H2O or any acid); deprotonation with NaH or other base and aqueous acid work-up; methylation using MeI and K2CO3 or any base to yield the final product or unmethylated diketone.
